entry.title: 🇵🇱 SAP CodeJam ABAP Cloud (Warsaw, Poland)
entry.content:
his SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of the ABAP Cloud Development Model in SAP S/4HANA.  

We will give you an introduction about the ABAP Cloud Development in an SAP S/4HANA on-premise environment which is available in SAP S/4HANA as of release 2022. The fundamental principle is that ABAP cloud-ready and ABAP classic code can coexist in the same system since the ABAP language version is defined on ABAP object level. This offers a gradual transition from the classic to the cloud extensibility model.

For this workshop we will provide you with access to system running on release SAP S/4HANA 2023 FSP01.

To understand the basic idea of ABAP Cloud Development you will learn how to:

- Use the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP) to develop transactional Fiori applications

- Use public SAP APIs released by SAP to access SAP functionality and SAP data

- Use public SAP extension points released by SAP to extend SAP objects

- Use ADT (ABAP Development Tools) as your ABAP IDE

With the SAP S/4HANA 2022 release the same extensibility model can now be used in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition, and SAP S/4HANA.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Friday 19  September 2025 in Warsaw Poland  from 02:00pm - 8:00pm local time. The language of the event and the content will be English. 

Location:  KMD Offices,  Inflancka 4A, Warsaw, Poland

The instructor is Ingo Braeuninger from SAP.

Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 30 attendees. Register here in the Community Event using the RSVP functionality.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Re: How to handle rounding differences when importing num...
entry.content: Read 3331676 - Group Reporting: Rounding selections.

entry.title: attaching a document to an unposted AP invoice
entry.content:
In SAP S4HANA Public Cloud is it possible to attach a document to an unposted invoice (Accounts Payable)

Thanks

entry.title: Enable SSO for BTP platform users
entry.content:
Hi all,

I’m trying to set up SSO with Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) for SAP BTP users in our subaccount. I’ve already:

  • Set up Entra ID with CSI as middelman

  • Successfully tested SSO via the app authentication URL

  • Users can log in to apps using Entra ID

However, I’m stuck on enabling SSO for platform users, users who need access to:

  • The SAP BTP Cockpit

  • Cloud Foundry spaces/orgs/roles

Right now, logging into the Cockpit only shows the default SAP ID Service (no Entra option).

 My questions:

  1. Is it possible to enable SSO (via Entra ID) for BTP platform users?

  2. Can I assign Entra users to Cloud Foundry spaces/orgs?

  3. Do I need IAS in between, or is direct Entra-to-BTP trust enough?

  4. Do I need to activate the Platform Identity Provider service?

Thanks in advance for any guidance or links!

entry.title: Re: Auto-populate Supplier Field in Asset Master When Ass...
entry.content:
Hi @ThanhHaLe ,

Public Cloud does not support automatic populating of the supplier from the PO into the asset master. Upon goods receipt, the system posts the acquisition correctly, but doesn't transfer PO supplier info into the asset master. There is no SSCUI or CBC configuration available to enable this.

Do it via customization. create a custom field (eg. ZSUPPLIER_PO) on Asset Master. Use Fiori -Manage Your Solution.
Add a Substitution Rule - while saving the asset, post-acquisition, if PO number exists, read PO header vendor via standard API, then populate supplier or your custom field. Display Custom Field in asset master Fiori view.

Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support.
If you find my response useful, I kindly request you to award kudos as a small gesture. This motivates me to serve you better.

Appreciate your feedback.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Restrict Tasks created in the Manage My Timesheet app
entry.content:
Hi,

 

In our employee time report, we can see time submitted by users using cost center and activity types only. We only need them to charge on WBS element or non project related task types. Is it possible to limit them on creating tasks using Cost center and activity type only? And how can we do that?

 

Best regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Restrict Tasks created in the Manage My Timesheet app
entry.content: In order to restrict users from submitting time for particular tasks, the BAdI HCM: Timesheet Validations (HCM_CATS_V1_TS_VALIDATION) can be implemented. Please see the following SAP Help documentation for more information on this App Adaptability and Extensibility: Manage My Timesheet.

entry.title: Re: New Expert-Guided Implementation: Exploring SAP SuccessFactors HCM Generative AI
entry.content:
Hi @alejandroazca ,

I would like to register to one of the sessions but unfortunately I'm not able to. When I click on the link, I'm just logged into my SF environment. I've also followed this video https://cdnapisec.kaltura.com/html5/html5lib/v2.91/mwEmbedFrame.php/p/1921661/uiconf_id/35919811/entry_id/1_59z2n8fc?wid=_1921661&iframeembed=true&playerId=kaltura_player_1195878186&entry_id=1_59z2n8fc but even with it I'm not able to do so. Could it be that SSO is causing issues for this? I've also used incognito window but I still have issues. I checked that my user is active and I'm receiving below error message when I try to do the last step explained in video

We couldn't log you in.

Please contact your system administrator.
Error: Invalid Login
Your account may be locked, inactive, or not included in the default user group. If your organization uses SSO, it may not be working correctly

entry.title: Re: Hello! to all members of community
entry.content:
Hello and welcome to SAP community Ronit! 

We're excited to have you here and I hope you have fun engaging with the people in the community. Make sure to check out our Manage Tags page. You can find lots of information about financial accounting there!

You can also look for your city among the Location Groups and join the events closest to you. If your city isn’t listed yet, you are more than welcome to start your own group.

entry.title: 🇧🇪 SAP CodeJam ABAP Cloud (Mechelen, Belgium)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of the ABAP Cloud, and specifically introduces attendees to ABAP Cloud and the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model. ABAP Cloud is the ABAP based development model for S/4HANA Cloud, S/4HANA on premise, and SAP BTP, ABAP environment. Learn how to use proven and new technologies, such as Core Data Services (CDS) and behavior definition and implementation languages, to build SAP Fiori apps and OData services on any of the ABAP Cloud options. In this hands-on experience, developers will create their first ABAP applications using ABAP Cloud, expose and consume services via HTTP, and explore the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Friday 19 September 2025 in Mechelen, Belgium  from 09:00 - 16:00 local time. The language of the event and the content will be English. 

Location:  Vaartdijk 86, 2800

The instructor is Andre Fischer

Here are essential prerequisites that must be completed by every attendee, before arriving at the event location. They are documented here: abap-exercises-codejam/prerequisites.md at main · SAP-samples/abap-exercises-codejam (github.com).

Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 25 attendees. Register here in the Community Event using the RSVP functionality.

**** ALSO!!!!   Make sure to register and attend the SAP InsideTrack event happening the very next day!!!  

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Find the Bug 🐞 Formatting dates and times in Build Apps
entry.content:
Someone sent me a problem they were having in a private message, that made me chuckle.

To be honest, after 2 years of war and 2 weeks of intense war, and having to live out of a suitcase for a week in Athens because my plane was diverted and could not enter my country -- this was a fun, amusing diversion (it actually made me laugh).

Here's the message I received:

 

FORMAT_DATETIME_WITH_TIMEZONE(DATETIME("2025-04-03T02:00:00.000Z"), "MMM d, yyyy", "America/Chicago")

 

"I am using formula to format the date. Actually the output should be Apr 2, 2025, 9 pm. But I am getting Apr 3, 2025, 9 pm. How to solve this? I tried multiple ways. It's only changing time but not date. I used other timezone format as well. Please help. This is an issue in production."

After running the formula in my Build Apps and getting the same result, I assumed it was a bug in the tool (@Mari and @Pekka_Aaltonen, please forgive me). For the programmer, the obvious thing to think was that you asked for the date in the format April 2 (because it had to give the day before because the time zone they gave was minus 6 hours from UTC) and instead it was giving April 3. Why? Probably because the formula was too stupid to return the previous day's date.

It was supposed to show this:

But kept showing this:

But, alas, there was a perfectly logical explanation, but it was hidden because of the actual date and time used in the example. Another case of all-too-human error.

Can you find the bug?

P.S.: If you like this puzzle, wait for Devtoberfest in October. We'll have plenty more.

entry.title: NA
entry.content: NA

entry.title: Re: Sap project system integration with production order
entry.content:
The best approach depends on the mix of production demand scenarios you have.  

Is your production all driven from a project or how much is project driven demand vs. MTO, MTS, S&OP demand?

A Network is an order type just like a PP Order, so there is a lot of overlapping functionality.

If you use Networks to perform production rather than PP orders you are disconnecting the 2 orders types.

I think a better fit would be to use PS Networks to create a project schedule and production demand into MRP and let MRP create the Production Order.  With this you have all of you Production Orders in one place and can be managed more effectively.

entry.title: Re: Hello SAP Community!
entry.content:
Welcome to the SAP Community, Sudeep!

It’s great to have you joining ys. Congratulations on completing your PGDERP in SAP FICO and best of luck with your internship at Daffodils!

Make sure to explore the Manage Tags  page to dive deeper into SAP FICO discussions, and don’t forget to check out the Location Groups  to connect with others in your area. If there’s no group for Ahmedabad yet, you’re more than welcome to start one!

Wishing you a great journey of learning and collaboration here!

entry.title: 🇩🇪 SAP CodeJam ABAP Cloud (Hamburg, Germany)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of the ABAP Cloud, and specifically introduces attendees to ABAP Cloud and the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model. ABAP Cloud is the ABAP based development model for S/4HANA Cloud, S/4HANA on premise, and SAP BTP, ABAP environment. Learn how to use proven and new technologies, such as Core Data Services (CDS) and behavior definition and implementation languages, to build SAP Fiori apps and OData services on any of the ABAP Cloud options. In this hands-on experience, developers will create their first ABAP applications using ABAP Cloud, expose and consume services via HTTP, and explore the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for  Thursday 25 September 2025 in Hamburg, Germany  from 09:00 - 16:00 local time. The language of the event and the content will be English. 

Location: TBD

The instructor is Ingo Braeuninger

Here are essential prerequisites that must be completed by every attendee, before arriving at the event location. They are documented here: abap-exercises-codejam/prerequisites.md at main · SAP-samples/abap-exercises-codejam (github.com).

Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 25 attendees. Register here in the Community Event using the RSVP functionality.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: *** Live sessions *** SAP Fiori - Lighthouse Apps in SAP S/4HANA - Register now
entry.content:
In this live session, we will explore the concept and value of Fiori Lighthouse Apps—strategic, next-generation SAP Fiori applications designed to deliver a modern, intuitive, and intelligent user experience in SAP S/4HANA. We will highlight key Lighthouse Apps across lines of business, and demonstrate their capabilities.

Learning objectives
After participating in this live session, you will have an understanding of:

  • What SAP Fiori Lighthouse Apps are and how they differ from classic Fiori apps
  • How Lighthouse Apps deliver innovation through intelligence and automation

Live Session schedule

Title Date / Time  
SAP Fiori - Lighthouse Apps in SAP S/4HANA
Aug 1, 2025, 08:00 - 10:00 GMT+2Register
SAP Fiori - Lighthouse Apps in SAP S/4HANA
Aug 1, 2025, 16:00 - 18:00 GMT+2Register

entry.title: Invalid receiver port when replicating G/L master data between two SAP systems via an IDOC flat file
entry.content:
I've got the following scenario which I want to run without custom program.

It currently fails on the last step, I hope you can propose a standard way to automate.

I've got two production S/4HANA systems C01 and D01, the master data from C01 must be replicated automatically on D01 every X days.

  1. On the system C01, I run the program RBDSER01 to replicate GLMAST messages via the group GRP_FI, which creates several outbound IDOC targeting D01 (one IDOC per G/L master created or changed since the last execution, the information is stored in the change pointers). A receiver file port has been configured. This port writes to a text file on a shared directory.
  2. I run these IDOC via BD87, they are successfully written to the file.
  3. On the system D01, I run the program RSEINB00 to process this text file, which creates several inbound IDOC, but their processing fails with status 56 and message "EDI: Receiver port in control record is invalid" (EA602) due to the receiver port. 

One workaround could have been to run WE12 to copy the file and change the target receiver port to SAPD01 (this works), but I cannot run it automatically.

Isn't there a simple way to integrate this IDOC text file automatically without having to modify it? If not, is there another solution?

Thanks.

Sandra

entry.title: Re: Connecting UI5 TypeScript Components locally
entry.content:
Hi,

this blog post is quite "old", but I used it to bind/use a TS SAPUI5 library in my application. The hint about the 'devDep..' in package.json was important. However, I still had issues using the library locally, even when using the ui5-middleware-ui5, as my app could not resolve the path. If you are struggling with the same problem, check the settings I configured in SAP BAS. These settings allow you to activate live reloading, meaning that when you change something in the library, your application will be refreshed automatically. This settings also enables you to use the library locally without building it every time or using server-static middleware to resolve the path from .js to .ts.

in manifest.json of the app:

"sap.ui5": {
    "flexEnabled": false,
    "dependencies": {
      "minUI5Version": "1.120.0",
      "libs": {
        "sap.m": {},
        "sap.ui.core": {},
        "xyz.glibutil": {
          "lazy": false
        }
      }
    }
 
in package.json of the app:
devDependencies": {
    "@sap-ux/eslint-plugin-fiori-tools": "^0.4.0",
    "@sap/ux-ui5-tooling": "1",
    "@sapui5/types": "~1.135.0",
    "@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin": "^7.1.1",
    "@typescript-eslint/parser": "^7.1.1",
    "@ui5/cli": "^3.0.0",
    "rimraf": "^5.0.5",
    "typescript": "^5.1.6",
    "ui5-tooling-transpile": "^3.3.7",
    "xyz.glibutil": "file:../xyz.glibutil"
  },
 
ui5.yaml of the app:
specVersion: "3.1"
metadata:
  name: my.little.app
type: application
server:
  customMiddleware:
    - name: fiori-tools-proxy
      afterMiddleware: compression
      configuration:
        ignoreCertError: false # If set to true, certificate errors will be ignored. E.g. self-signed certificates will be accepted
        ui5:
          path:
            - /resources
            - /test-resources
          url: https://ui5.sap.com
        backend:
          - path: /sap
            destination: XXX
            url: http://xxx
    - name: fiori-tools-appreload
      afterMiddleware: compression
      configuration:
        port: 35729
        path:
          - "webapp"
          - "../xyz.glibutil"
        delay: 300
    - name: fiori-tools-preview
      afterMiddleware: fiori-tools-appreload
      configuration:
        component: my.little.app
        ui5Theme: sap_horizon
    - name: ui5-tooling-transpile-middleware
      afterMiddleware: compression
      configuration:
        debug: true
        transpileDependencies: true
        transformModulesToUI5:
          overridesToOverride: true
        excludePatterns:
          - /Component-preload.js
builder:
  customTasks:
    - name: ui5-tooling-transpile-task
      afterTask: replaceVersion
      configuration:
        debug: true
        transpileDependencies: true
        transformModulesToUI5:
          overridesToOverride: true

entry.title: Re: SAC/Seamless Planning – Transactions Recorded at High...
entry.content:
Hi Nikhil,

thank you very much for your answer. Just to clarify, did you mean that I should create a second hierarchy within my dimension where all elements are treated as leaf nodes? Or did I possibly misunderstand your suggestion? I tried to create a second hierarchy in the dimension, but I encountered the following error message: “You cannot use the current member as a parent.” Could you please provide more details on how exactly the alternative hierarchy should be structured? That would be really helpful. Thanks again for your support!

Best regards.

entry.title: Re: Configuration for secure act 2.0 section 109 roll ove...
entry.content:
@joe_jones2,

We’re in the same boat!  We’ve looked at note 3365473, but the options are lacking:

  • WRNG_SKIP & SKIP if you want to make your catch-up employees mad
  • ERROR if you want to irritate your Payroll Department
  • WRNG_CALC and CALC if you want to flout the legislation

The SKIP options do move the would-be catch-up amount into wage type /TB8, so I suppose you could do something with that downstream in the payroll schema.  I hope SAP works on this a bit more to deliver a complete solution.  The Regulatory Change Manager hints at something to be delivered 8/1/25, so maybe there’s hope…  https://app.main.regulatory-change-manager.cloud.sap/index.html#/detail/GSREQAMER-11581_8

entry.title: Re: Identity and Access Management with Microsoft Entra, Part I: Managing access to SAP BTP
entry.content: Hi @MSo the help link lists the steps for pulling data from leading Identity source (Entra ID)  using SAP IPS to the target BTP solution.  This works well including the substitution of the Global User ID in IAS directory using Global user ID from Entra ID.   The issue is with using Entra ID provisioning engine to achieve the same result - there is no direct involvement of IPS here.

entry.title: Relationship of product id with pricelist in sap crm. how to check this in config level
Re: attaching a document to an unposted AP invoice
entry.content:
Hi @JoanneDuggan ,

Yes, you can attach a document in AP. At the bottom of your app - Create supplier Invoice - you have "Attachments" option. Use that to upload pdf or image. The attachment will get saved when you post the document. Attachment will also be retained even though you haven't posted the invoice yet.

Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support.
If you find my response useful, I kindly request you to award kudos as a small gesture. This motivates me to serve you better.

Appreciate your feedback.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Invalid receiver port when replicating G/L master dat...
entry.content:
It will only be handled automatically on import if the receiver port is blank, which means you would need to alter what is output somehow, or adjust the file after it is generated - 

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Cannot make ITPPM_PROJECT UI Elements Invisible
entry.content:
Hello,

We are implementing ChaRM and we are not using ITPPM. We have therefore attempted to hide the ITPPM Project related fields that are available by standard in Standard Change documents (ZMSG), but even after adjusting the configuration as required (see attachment for details), the fields still appear.

Requesting your support and guidance.

Thanks

entry.title: Send OData Batch Requests in SAP Build Apps (JSON)
entry.content:
Many people have asked about sending OData batch requests via SAP Build Apps. It can be done, if your API accepts JSON-formatted payloads for batch requests.

Just use the HTTP Destination Request flow function.

Then set the following:

  • Destination: Create an integration with your destination that points to your API (no need to specify any entities).
  • Method: POST
  • Body Type: JSON
  • Request Body: Just define a page variable with the JSON schema you need, and set the values with a Set Page Variable flow function. More on this below.
  • Path: /$batch
  • Headers: Content-Type = application/json

Request Body

The request body would be something like this (here we are create new records):

{
    "requests": [
        {
            "id": "1",
            "method": "POST",
            "url": "/Orders",
            "headers": {
                "content-type": "application/json; odata.metadata=minimal; odata.streaming=true",
                "odata-version": "4.0"
            },
            "body": {
                "customer": "JSON1",
                "date": "2021-06-30T00:00:00Z",
                "status": "Initiated",
                "total": 99,
                "currency_code": "USD"
            }
        },
        {
            "id": "2",
            "method": "POST",
            "url": "/Orders",
            "headers": {
                "content-type": "application/json; odata.metadata=minimal; odata.streaming=true",
                "odata-version": "4.0"
            },
            "body": {
                "customer": "JSON2",
                "date": "2021-06-30T00:00:00Z",
                "status": "Initiated",
                "total": 100,
                "currency_code": "USD"
            }
        }
    ]
}

For my test, I already had the JSON above so I just put it in a JavaScript component, parsed it, and output it.

But you could create a page variable, and in your app populate the variable with your data. Then you could use this page variable for the request body.

Unfortunately, I could not find a way to use the more traditional multipart/mixed payload format from SAP Build Apps. If you can find a way, let us know.

entry.title: Re: How to connect my BTP Trial ABAP Account to BAS Trial...
entry.content:
In BAS, go to terminal and use following command, you can get the cloud foundry URL from your BTP account.

cf login -a https://api.cf.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com

entry.title: Re: How to create a RAP BO to change the content of a customizing table in the production client

Hello Patrick,

Thanks for the quick response.

The solution is working fine for me now.

I have below questions:

2.Do we need to Transport 3579012 to Production environment or higher environmnents ?

3.Delete the generated transport object. I didnt do this step, but still it works. I selected 'No Transport' when creating BC maintenance object. is this step required in that case ?

Thanks,

Manjunath

entry.title: Re: SAP AI Core & SAP AI Launchpad - a visual introduction (Part 1)
entry.content:

Does Launchpad provides a REST API, terraform module or any SDK, so that I can add workspace to it programmatically?Thank you.

entry.title: problem connecting process automation with build app
entry.content:
Hi, 

I have build an business process automation, and created a destination 

sap_process_automation_service_user_access with following properties:

sap.cloud.service = com.sap.spa.processautomation

sap.cloud.service.alias = spa

saasregistryenabled = true

MobileEnabled = true

HTML5.DynamicDestination = true

CEP.EnableCsrfProtection = true

sap.applicationdevelopment.actions.enabled = true

sap.processautomation.enabled = true

WebIDEEnabled = true

AppgyverEnabled = true

endpoints = blank

html5-apps-repo = blank

In the buildApp  App setting, BTP authentication is configured.   The process automation is released and published.  However, in the buildApp Integration section, the process automation is not shown.  Am I missing anything?  Is there anything else I need to set up/check?  I'd appreciate any guidance on the issue.  Thanks in advance.  

 

Lily

entry.content: What was the error message and how did it occur?

entry.title: Re: Invalid receiver port when replicating G/L master dat...
entry.content: Thanks. Empty receiver port or SAP<SID> (what I did). An empty receiver port is more simple and it's accepted by WE12. Is there a standard way to achieve the scenario automatically, i.e. without custom program and human intervention?

entry.title: Re: EWM Customizing missing after S4 Brownfield Conversion
entry.content: The documentation of the IMG nodes for warehouse request document and item types (for example 'Define Document Types for Inbound Delivery' contain a link to the available BC sets.

entry.title: Using RAP action for synchronous commiting processing (Another BO and outside of LUW)
entry.content:
Hello Community,

We're currently building a solution based on RAP unmanaged (OData V4 & on-premise 2023 FPS3) + OData V4 + Fiori Elements List Reports with multiple view entityset (for SAP BUILD Workzone) — around 10 tiles depending on user profiles.
With the goal of being cloud compliant (that's why we exluded SEGW).

Our backend scenario doesn’t follow a typical RAP-unmanaged BO:
We don’t have a persistent Z-table representing the main BO. Instead, we expose a merged view of:

  • Sales Orders,
  • Purchase Orders,
  • A custom Z-table that manages additional status, dates on them. And is updated both by UI and interfaces (IDocs, API, etc.).

Each List Report is tied to a projection CDS/behavior with specific actions available (from main behavior): Approve, Reject, Confirm, etc.
Some complex Actions must be executed synchronously to orchestrate multiple steps, (committing or rollback ourselves depending rules between step) and return structured information (e.g., results & messages) to the UI. The frontend expects instant feedback and reacts to message type (S, W, E...) to guide the user. Example sequences :

  • Update standard document via BAPI PO/SO
    (No use of standard RAP due to doc type not herited from NBF and some fields not managed by current RAP),
  • Update Ztable via EML (RAP),
  • Trigger external APIs.

 

🚫  Problem with RAP standard actions

We initially built RAP actions (with deep parameter) that return a complex response (deep result), for manage specifically result & messages in Fiori side. However, we quickly hit one RAP limitation :

  • You cannot commit or rollback inside an action.
    As it breaks RAP’s design principles (LUW control, Clean Core).

So we tried using cl_abap_parallel (released class) or even function modules with DESTINATION 'NONE', which technically allowed us to run parts of the logic in a separate LUW and return structured results synchronously.

And see some others Posts using these kind of workaround solution.

👌  This completely solved our issues at runtime — enabling orchestration, feedback, and proper error control.

However, after discussing with SAP, we were clearly told this is not a long-term solution.
Such techniques are discouraged as they break the RAP LUW model and violate Clean Core principles.

 

💡  SAP suggested:

Firstly :

"Trigger this logic in SAVE sequence via BGPF"

We did a full POC of this model and found several issues for our case:

  • No possibility to simulate during MODIFY runtime (action) before SAVE (to manage deep result at this time)
    You can't do a commit entity in simulation mode to know validation problems etc on our ztable rap. And some BAPI like SO can't be executed in simulation mode (due to authority-check triggered that can't be deactivate like PO BAPI)
  • COMMIT ENTITY (of another BO!) inside BGPF crashes
  • The user doesn’t get immediate feedback in UI
  • Save have only Reported return. Instead of action that can manage a deep result
  • We’d have to rework the entire UX logic (tracking status of BGPF per order to lock user action, error reprocess design, etc.)
  • Event RAP Side effects are not available in our current release (2023 FPS03. Available in 2025).
    From an API-only scenario, Side effects is not an option

Secondly :

Falling back to OData V2 SEGW (only for the action) and use Function Import POST — which gives us exactly what we need: a self-contained synchronous LUW with a structured response!

But as you know, SEGW is not released, so we aren't totally cloud compliante.

Today, this is the solution implemented. It kind of takes the soul out of our RAP implementation, which is frustrating 😅

 

👨‍🔬 Question to the community

  • How have you solved similar challenges?

  • @Andre_Fischer if you have time, we would really appreciate your insights on this case.

We’ve created a SAP Influence post to support this request → https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/351633/?section=sectionDetails

Have a nice day.

Clément

entry.title: Re: Cloud Economics (aka FinOps) with SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hi,

Thank you for sharing this interesting information.

I am happy to share the enhanced FinOps capabilities in SAP BTP to better control, manage and optimize cloud spending via Budget Management with Alerts and Cloud Credit Forecasting.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/enhanced-finops-capabilities-for-sap-btp/ba-p/14113797

Best regards,

Keren

entry.title: Re: Standard apps add custom button in public cloud
entry.content:
Hi @nirojan_vel ,

Check app’s backend model via /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE, if the app is RAP-based, use Developer Extensibility in ADT or Eclipse, add custom action to behavior definition. UI will render a button automatically. If not RAP-based, build a custom app using released CDS view.

Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support.
Appreciate your feedback.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Seeking Architectural Guidance for a Generic Data Maintenance Tool on BW/4HANA – RAP, SEGW, CAP
entry.content: Can you elaborate your needs? Is it custom tables or SAP tables? Which BW version?

entry.title: Re: Unable to Rename Apps in SAP Build Apps - Free Edition
entry.content:
After a tiresome search all over the internet (or at least as far as I could find), I came to the conclusion that the only way to rename it is to actually download a copy of the app (exporting it from the home page) and create a new one by importing that copy. I had to rename the local file to the name I wanted and also define the same new name when creating the new app. That was the only way it worked.

It's a shame there isn't an option as basic as this.

entry.title: Re: SAP Sybase ASE Audit Logs: Handling Multi-Row Single...
entry.content:
Have you tried using the sequence column in sysaudits_xx?  

 

SELECT extrainfo FROM sysaudits_xx where eventtime =x and spid=x ORDER BY SEQUENCE

entry.title: Re: attaching a document to an unposted AP invoice
entry.content: I hope you're using the modern “Create Supplier Invoice” app (F0859A) not the old one. It has a dedicated attachments section. If you're not seeing this app, it may be due to your assigned business role. You might need business catalog SAP_AP_APP_COMMON and SAP_BR_AP_ACCOUNTANT

entry.title: Re: Spreadsheet Export in SU53 now displays in German text
entry.content: SE80 did the trick. Went into the Program path> GUI Status> TREE and updated text from there. Appreciate the help, Divyesh!

entry.title: Re: Becoming an SAP BTP Functional Consultant in 2025 - A Practical Guide to Thrive Beyond ECC &&&&
entry.content:
@PRAT0302 
I really appreciate the support, and I hope you found the post valuable.

entry.title: Master Data Replication: Creation of Communication Arrangement SAP_COM_0659
entry.content:
Prerequisites

Before you create a communication arrangement, go to SAP BTP Cockpit, select the respective service instance, and copy the service binding 

How to copy Service key (or) binding

 Go to BTP :MDI subaccount and follow the below steps

Step1: go to MDI subaccount 

step2: Click Service binding from the instance

Step3 : select json mode and click copy json 

you have copied the service key

Procedure

Automated Creation

  1. Log on to the SAP Fiori launchpad in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system.

  2. Select the Communication Arrangement app.

  3. Click New and in the New Communication Arrangement dialog, select the scenario SAP_COM_0659.

  4. Paste the service binding that you copied from the SAP BTP Cockpit. (Paste the  json)

  5. Choose Create.

  6. Verify the auto-filled details:

    • Arrangement Name

    • Communication System

    • Outbound Communication

    • Outbound Services

  7. Save your communication arrangement.
 

Regards

Janarthanan

#SAP_COM_0659 #SAP Master Data Integration

entry.title: Re: Invalid receiver port when replicating G/L master dat...
entry.content: @Sandra_RossiI think the stumbling block you are hitting with this setup is the use of a file port. My guess is you are seeing SAPC01 as the receiver port because with a file port the sending system is still the receiver. One thing you could try is creating a dummy XML HTTP port named SAPD01 that is responsible for taking the IDoc payloads in and recording them wherever they need to be placed... effectively a web application wrapper.

entry.title: Re: SAP B1 BY-PRODUCT
entry.content:
Hi @mari_outtaleb96 ,

Do you have a specific Item Master for this By-Product?
Is it stored in a different warehouse?
Does it need to have a cost?

Sending some screenshots would be helpful as well.

BR,
Felipe

entry.title: Re: New Expert-Guided Implementation: Exploring SAP SuccessFactors HCM Generative AI
entry.content:
Hi @laura_li ,

Valid S-user is required to sign up for SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support. The access to can be requested in this link.   

Please note that the assignment process to our edition takes up to 2 hours until you receive a welcome email.  Once you receive the welcome email you´ll be able to register in the EGI.

Can you please try to register in the EGI again?

Best regards

Alejandro

Best regards

Alejandro

entry.title: Re: Invalid receiver port when replicating G/L master dat...
entry.content:
You're probably right. I guess a file port doesn't behave exactly the same as HTTP or RFC ports. A file port is not intended to connect directly two SAP systems. My scenario is special as the two SAP systems are on different networks due to security. In fact, I said there was a shared directory to simplify the scenario, but actually the file must be transferred via a physical support.

NB: I let the discussion open for a few days in case someone else come with other possibilities.

entry.title: Re: Make JobInfo Field Mandatory
entry.content: Have you tried adding a business rule for the field?

entry.title: Validating the Valuation Class in Material Master MM01
entry.content:
Hello, nice to meet you.
When creating the material master in MM01, the user enters incorrect valuation classes (BKLAS). Is there any validation?
Can we control this?

For example: we have the plant code here, A, B, C, D, E, and F are plants.

Plant/Value Class CC (BKLAS)
un 1001
un 1002
B 2001
B 2002
C 3001
C 3002
He enters valuation class A for company code B, for example. Can we control this when creating the material master?

I would like to know if there is any way to restrict the valuation class by plant or by user (role).

Please help.
Best regards,

entry.title: SAP BusinessObjects BI 2029 is confirmed
entry.content:
A Clear Commitment to the Future

We’re excited to share some important news with our SAP BusinessObjects community:
the BI 2029 release is officially confirmed.

Following the successful delivery of BI 2025 in March 2025, and with BI 2027 already planned, this announcement brings long-term clarity and continuity to our roadmap.

BI 2029 isn’t just a version number. It’s a strong signal to all our customers and partners that SAP BusinessObjects continues to evolve. Many of you have made significant investments in your BI landscape, confirming BI 2029 today means these investments are safe, supported, and ready for the future.

 

Plan Ahead with Confidence

Our commitment is simple: we want you to plan with confidence.

Having a roadmap visibility until at least end of 2031 for SAP BusinessObjects helps our customers secure budgets, define internal strategies, and align BI platforms with IT governance. This visibility also opens the door for meaningful upgrades and perspectives. If you're still on an older version, now is a good time to move forward, whether it's upgrading your on-premise system or migrating to SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), for example.

BI 2025 brought significant updates and innovations, and these foundations will be further refined in future releases such the BI 2027 and the BI 2029.

 

Use Cases That Matter

Since the release of BI 2025, we’ve seen strong momentum around enhancements that directly support real needs. Whether you're using Web Intelligence, the BI Platform, or Crystal Reports, the focus has remained the same: simplify, modernize, and deliver trust.

In Web Intelligence, BI 2025 introduced dynamic dashboards, new layout plus interactive widgets, and the Web intelligence Document eXtract (WIDX) export format for offline sharing of interactive content. Business users can now build engaging dashboards without relying on the IT department. This makes it easier to share insights with teams or executives who need quick access, even without logging into the platform.

In the BI Platform, a more flexible upgrade path is now proposed. It also adds the ability to pin KPIs and charts directly to the homepage of the BI Launch Pad. This reduces change management efforts and provides faster access to critical metrics, ideal for operational monitoring.

With Crystal Reports, enhancements enable smooth migration from CR4E with better UNX and BW DSL support, easing transitions while preserving report logic. New database-side sorting and ranking improve performance when working with large datasets. And the redesigned Desktop Viewer boosts offline access and interactivity, ideal for casual or mobile users

Each of these features reflects real use cases: business users building interactive dashboards, IT teams managing large-scale deployments, or analysts delivering pixel-perfect reports for regulators.

Customers continue to rely on BusinessObjects for exactly this. These aren’t just features: they’re everyday scenarios where BusinessObjects delivers real value. BI 2029 will build on this foundation, helping organizations do more with their data without changing the tools they already trust.

 

More details

Official SAP Analytics Statement of Direction (SoD).

Welcome to SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025, Modern BI for a data-driven world.

 

Thank you for your continued trust in SAP BusinessObjects

We look forward to the road ahead. Do not hesitate to share your thoughts and ideas via a comment.

entry.title: Unable to duplicate EDI messages - "Manage Output Items"
entry.content:
Hello all, 

We are using SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and would like to clarify the expected behavior regarding output processing across the following apps: 

1. "Duplicate" function for EDI outputs in the "Manage Output Items" app

In the "Manage Output Items" app, the "Duplicate" button is visible and active for EDI output messages. 

However, when attempting to duplicate an EDI message, the system returns a message indicating that this action is not allowed for EDI channel. Ate the same time, we are able to manually add a new output line, with the same output type and channel EDI, in the "Change Billing Documents" app (this is done by manually creating a new row in the output section of the billing document - not via the "Duplicate" option). 

Is there any supported method to enable duplication of EDI outputs?

2. Retry in "Manage Output Items"

When an output message is in "Error" status and we select "Retry" in the "Manage Output Items" app, the system directly updates the status to Completed. As a result, the "Send" button is no longer available, and we are unable to reprocess or resend the message from this app. However, when retrying the same type of output via the "Change Billing Documents" app, the system sets the status "To Be Output", which allows proper reprocessing and sending through the usual process. 

Can the retry logic in "Manage Output Items" be configured to behave like it does in "Change Billing Documents"?

Thank you in advance for any guidance.

Hello all,

I'm Koichi.  I recently started working on SAP Business One.  My role is QA with the software development team of my company that has many shops and offices in Mexico.  Will be learning modules and functionalities of SAP B1 for better understanding of my QA evaluations.

Nirojan.

entry.title: Re: Enhanement on BP REL Handler class : MDG
entry.content:
@karthik_subramanian Hello Karthik, kindly confirm the my understanding below: 

  1. Your requirement is to filter the data using relationship category.
  2. You do not want to filter the data post the select query.

In this case, instead of using implicit enhancement, can you not use over-write exit in the method to manipulate the select query and add the filter of the relationship category as a part of the where clause? Think about it from this perspective!
Although, I would really like to understand why can't you use the filter post the select query?

Regards,
Saikat

entry.title: Re: can i write custom entity with Governance API?
entry.content:
Yes, you can! 
However, be cautious about instantiating the governance API. 
Read through the MDG API Guide to understand the flow.

https://www.sap.com/documents/2015/07/666e4158-5b7c-0010-82c7-eda71af511fa.html

Regards,
Saikat.

Hi Friends,

System is not accepting Table : KNVV as Result table when i specify input Table : BUT0IS . 

Any body have any idea. Please let me know. 

 

Thanks,

Balaji.T.

entry.title: Process for Manual Completion of the Open Deliveries
entry.content:
Manual Completion of the open deliveries process when the EWM delivery is rejected and queue fails to process from EWM to S4HANA

Delivery:

  • A delivery document represents the movement of goods, either inbound (receipts) or outbound (shipments).
  • It triggers warehouse activities such as picking, packing, and goods issue/posting.
  • Deliveries are essential for linking sales/purchase orders to logistics execution processes.

EWM (Extended Warehouse Management):

  • SAP EWM manages complex warehouse operations with real-time visibility and control.
  • It supports advanced features like slotting, labor management, and automation integration.
  • EWM handles warehouse tasks based on delivery documents from S/4HANA.

S/4HANA:

  • S/4HANA is SAP's next-generation ERP suite built on the in-memory HANA database.
  • It offers a simplified data model, modern UX (Fiori), and real-time analytics.
  • Core business processes like Finance, Logistics, and Manufacturing are seamlessly integrated.

Queue:

  • Queues in SAP (especially in EWM integration) manage asynchronous data transfers between systems.
  • They help ensure reliable communication when messages cannot be processed immediately.
  • Errors in queues (e.g., stuck deliveries) must be monitored and resolved to ensure data consistency.

 

Step to perform the Manual Completion of the open deliveries process when the EWM delivery is rejected and queue fails to process from EWM to S4HANA

Step1: 

Create an outbound delivery using VL01N transaction code.

 

Step2:

Outbound delivery is distributed to EWM.

Then reject the delivery using the process code.

 

Step3:

Queue fails to process from EWM to S4HANA

 

Step4:

Since the status shows as “Not yet” started in S4 delivery.

Navigate to the tcode “VL_COMPLETE“ to manually complete the open delivery.

Provide the below details:

Delivery

Created On Date

And then execute.

Then select the delivery line and then process it by click on the flag icon.

A dialog box appears as “Adjust Delivery Status”. Then select the option “Yes”.

The goods movement status is changed from not started (A) to completed (C).

Delivery successfully processed is displayed.

 

Step5: 

The good movement status is changed to completed in the S4 delivery.

Conclusion:

Manual completion of the open deliveries process when the EWM delivery is rejected and the queue fails to process from EWM to S4HANA is provided.

entry.title: Re: Output Type SPER Delete Inbound Delivery After We Reverse PGI for STO' outbound Delivery
entry.content: I am getting this error for the Catch weight related deliveries . can you please assist

entry.title: Re: SAC/Seamless Planning – Transactions Recorded at High...
entry.content: You need to create a second hierarchy where all parent members (only) from first hierarchy are leaf under a parent node. You can create something like TOT_ALL as a member and make all parent nodes from first hierarchy as child of this new member.

entry.title: Re: Creating a Supplier Hierarchy ?
entry.content:
@titu4mdg Hello, nice to e-meet you!

Supplier hierarchies are different as compared to BP Hierarchies. 
The same functionality is not available out-of-the-box with SAP MDG-BP.
In order to maintain supplier hierarchies in SAP MDG, you will need to create a custom solution for maintaining the same - this could be done with custom data model enhancement of BP data model to accommodate the supplier hierarchy assignment functionality.

I would suggest you go through the transaction codes such as MKH1, MKH2 and see how the functionality works.
Also, explore the option with business if they can maintain this set of data outside of SAP MDG i.e. via transaction code access.

Below are some of the important documentations that might help you design the same:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/9905622a5c1f49ba84e9076fc83a9c2c/7496c7536e8e2a4be10000000a174cb4.html?q=Supplier+Hierarchy 

Regards,
Saikat

entry.title: Re: B/S Reclassification starts to use reversal posting logic from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: How to do a cube to cube join in Datasphere?
entry.content:
I have two data models - Cube A & Cube B. I don't see any option to add cubes (RL Layer of a model) into graphical view.
Is there any other way I can join two datasphere analytical models / cubes together? Any help document would be really appreciated!

Thanks!

entry.title: Sap Analytics Cloud - Security for an end user to be able to upload data using an upload job
entry.content:
Hello, 

I'm having issues configuring the role for the end user to be able to upload data to the Planning Model through an upload job. 

I have the user with the Modeler role and the next things on: 

he following permissions are required to import data into a model:

  • Create permission on either Analytic Model or Planning Model.
  • Execute permission on Personal Data Acquisition.
    => Without this permission, you cannot choose option "Import a file from your computer" to create a new model.
  • Create and Read permissions on Connection.
    => Without the two permission, you will get error message "The upload failed due to an unexpected internal server error. Please try again later. If the problem persists, please contact your system administrator" when uploading file.
  • Create permission on Dimension.
    => Without this permission, you will get error message like "Model <dimension name> creation has failed. <...> "error":"You are not authorized to Create Dimension <dimension name>."" when creating model.
  • Users also need the Read and Maintain model permissions to upload/change data for existing models. See Model and Version Security and Model Administration Scenarios.
  • Users need permissions in two places in order to perform actions on a model: they need the general application permission (via role) to Read, Update, Delete, or Maintain analytic or planning models, and they also need the Read, Update, Delete, or Maintain permission granted via the Sharing Settings directly on the model itself.

But the upload job is still rejecting all the data due to data access control.

I also have the users in the read and write dimmension: Audti trail, business area and company. Dimmensions that in my model have security on. 

Any ideas what can be happening? 

 

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Automating U.S. Sales & Use Tax Calculation with External Partner Integration in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content: @IBSabrina  This interface is for public cloud only and for U.S. only.

entry.title: Re: Automating U.S. Sales & Use Tax Calculation with External Partner Integration in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content: @szhanden no need to create tax code, there are 5 pre-delived tax codes in scope item 4Q2

entry.title: Re: Implementação da NT 2024.003
entry.content:
Boa tarde, Carla.

Estava com o mesmo problema, porém, estamos migrando do ECC para o S/4HANA, mesmo assim, pode ser que seja a mesma situação.

Realizei duas alterações:

1 - No grupo de funções J1BB2, no Layout da tela 2000, deletei a tab Agriculture and Livestock que já havia sido criada, porém, estava com o número de linha errado. Criei novamente com as mesmas características, como a última tab, no grupo TAB, código de função TAGR e Cpo.ref. HEADER_TAB. Ficou com linha 1 e coluna 15.

 

2 - SPRO -> Componentes válidos para várias aplicações -> Funções gerais de aplicação -> Nota Fiscal - Nota fiscal - administração de documentos -> Definir controles tela p/fichas de registro e subdivisões tela (cabeçalho):

Cliquei nos controles de tela existentes para editar e e marquei "Exibir" para o grupo de tela 14, referente à aba AGRICULTURE.

 

Após esses passos, a aba ficou disponível nas telas J1B*N.

 

Atenciosamente!

entry.title: S/4HANA On-premises and S/4HANA private cloud
entry.content:
Dear all,

I am already working on public cloud and I am looking for best practices of on-premises manufacturing module for a customer demo (not private cloud) but I cannot find anything. All scope items are now turned into private cloud and there is no t-codes anymore in the scope items.

This is correct or I am searching in a total wrong correction? Because as far as I know, in private cloud, it is possible to use lovely GUI screens with transactions and table. Maybe SAP pushing new generation toward browser usage instead of GUI use?

Br,

Adventurer

entry.title: S/4HANA On-premises and S/4HANA private cloud
entry.content:
Dear all,

I am already working on public cloud and I am looking for best practices of on-premises manufacturing module for a customer demo (not private cloud) but I cannot find anything. All scope items are now turned into private cloud and there is no t-codes anymore in the scope items.

This is correct or I am searching in a total wrong correction? Because as far as I know, in private cloud, it is possible to use lovely GUI screens with transactions and table. Maybe SAP pushing new generation toward browser usage instead of GUI use?

Br,

Adventurer

entry.title: Use SAP Cloud ALM Cloud ALM with third party test automation software for execution and reporting
entry.content:
Hey,
I would like to use SAP Cloud ALM as reporting tool for my test automation projects in a 3th party app (eg: Katalon). I know there are standard API's of SAP Cloud ALM but I cannot find instructions on how to use these with a 3th party app. This should cover both solutions:

  1. Creation & Execution of a test (via SAP Cloud ALM -> third party test automation software)
  2. Reporting within SAP Cloud ALM

Can you deliver the way of working with a third party test automation tool (similar to Tricentis)?

Thanks in advance!


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Use SAP Cloud ALM Cloud ALM with third party test aut...
entry.content:
Follow the Integrating Other Test Automation Providers guide on the SAP Help Portal. Note, your third party Test Automation software can only be integrated with SAP Cloud ALM if it implements the public Test Automation API. If it does not implement this API, it cannot be integrated with SAP Cloud ALM.

The Analytics for Testing in SAP Cloud ALM are driven by SAP Cloud ALM's actual involvement in the Testing process. The analytics are only updated when SAP Cloud ALM is involved in executing the Test Executions in the Test Automation Provider. The Analytics in SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation (which includes Testing) are not standalone dashboards, they are driven by the actual interactions that SAP Cloud ALM has in the process. 

If you integrate your third party software with SAP Cloud ALM and use SAP Cloud ALM to trigger the test executions etc., then the Analytics apps should get updated with that data. But as far as I can tell, there is no way to pull historical data (for older projects and executions) into SAP Cloud ALM from the Test Automation provider. 

For more information refer to KBA 3628967 - Connecting SAP Cloud ALM to third party/external Test Automation software

entry.title: Re: Become Part of the SAP TechEd Family as a Community Talk Speaker!
entry.content:
Hi,

if I select Track Application Development and Automation only AI-driven-Subtracks are available. And only one is ABAP related.

Is there also a classic track - just for ABAP?

Regards, Johann

entry.title: Customer ARM scenario where inbound delivery order from SAP EWM not showing up in FO workflow
entry.content:
We are trying to create a standard customer return scenario with basic embedded TM. Advanced return management is being used for customer returns.

On the TM side ASR is activated. Delivery based FU and Inbound delivery order are created. Inbound delivery order gets blocked for Warehouse processing. This document does not show up in workflow of TM.

The Freight order is created and all steps done but the integration with EWM inbound delivery order from EWM is not happening.

Does somebody have the exact configuration steps from SAP TM and EWM side. What is the standard inbound delivery document type used in SAP EWM in this scenario.

entry.title: Re: Manage Purchase Order app does not show custom docume...
entry.content: Custom document types with field selection key "NBF" are supported in the FIORI app "Manage Purchase Orders". Change the PO document type setting as mentioned.

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch / SAP Community Meetup Dortmund 30.07.2025
entry.content:
Der nächste SAP Stammtisch Dortmund findet am 30. Juli 2025 ab 18:30 Uhr statt.

Eingeladen ist jeder, der Lust und Zeit hat, unabhängig vom fachlichen Hintergrund, von Entwickler, Berater, Projekt Manager, Basis Administrator bis hin zum Endanwender. Im Mittelpunkt steht ein lockerer fachlicher Austausch rund um SAP-Themen über Unternehmensgrenzen hinweg.

Wir freuen uns über bekannte und neue Gesichter. Melde dich idealerweise bis zum 23.07. an, damit wir passend planen können. Aber auch eine spontane Teilnahme ist gern gesehen.

Wann? 30.07.2025, 18:30 Uhr
Wo? Hövels Hausbrauerei, Hoher Wall 5-7, 44135 Dortmund

Weitere Infos findest du auch auf unserer Webseite: SAP Stammtisch Dortmund

Bis dann!

---

Our next SAP Community Meetup Dortmund will take place on July 30th 2025, at 06:30 PM.

Everyone is invited, regardless of their professional background, from developers, consultants, project managers, basis administrators to end users. The focus is on a relaxed professional exchange about SAP topics across company boundaries.

We look forward to seeing familiar and new faces. Ideally, please RSVP by the 23rd so that we can plan accordingly. However, spontaneous participation is also welcome.

When? July 30th 2025, 6:30 PM
Where? Hövels Hausbrauerei, Hoher Wall 5-7, 44135 Dortmund

You can find additional information on our website (German): SAP Stammtisch Dortmund

See you then!

entry.title: How to avoid class/time slots overlapping error in a program?
entry.content:
I have a program that has items with classes attached to them - the class date/time needs to be the same for all the classes - I am getting the error that my classes are overlapping  - is there a workaround?

Item number 1000/Class 1 - date/time = 01-JUL-2025 8-10am

Item number 2000/Class 2 - date/time = 01-JUL-2025 8-10am

Item number 3000/Class 3 - date/time = 01-JUL-2025 8-10am

Thank you

entry.title: Can we disable "Training Reminder Almost Due" e-mail notification?
entry.content: I have the learning expiration email notification enabled to run monthly with a threshold of 30.  However, an almost due notification is also sent out.  How can I find and/or disable the almost due notification?  Thanks for any help.

entry.title: SAP Commerce Cloud Q2 ‘25 Release Highlights
entry.content:
In the "2025 State of B2B eCommerce Report," written in collaboration with MasterB2B, many organizations expressed challenges with transitioning from analog-focused operations to digital-centric models. The difficulty is compounded by a lack of digital expertise and specialization among many companies. 

 We understand these challenges, and in Q2 2025 we introduced numerous B2B commerce improvements, including the announcement of the B2B Self-Service Portal at SAP Sapphire, designed to support organizations in easing into this digital transformation and meeting growing B2B customer expectations. 

We invite you to explore these B2B enhancements and other important SAP Commerce Cloud updates from the last three months by joining our Q2 2025 Release Highlights webcast on July 8 to stay updated on our latest advancements. A replay will be available afterwards. 

 

Adaptive 


Accelerate the growth of your B2B commerce business quickly 
 SAP B2B Self-Service Portal is an SAP Cloud ERP add-on designed to provide customers with post-purchase transparency without the time, effort, or expense involved in a comprehensive commerce implementation. Customers can start small and launch in a matter of weeks using partner implementation packages. You can later expand to SAP Commerce Cloud, all without requiring data or environment migration. 

Buyers benefit by receiving real-time access to order information, delivery status, and invoices, reducing the load on inside sales and support teams. This allows your teams to focus on building relationships and value-generating tasks, leading to higher customer satisfaction and lower overall service costs. 

B2B Self-Service Portal 

Enhance the purchasing experience for B2B buyers 
 

SAP Commerce Cloud, Open Payment Framework launched in phases starting in Q4 2024, initially focusing on B2C payment processes. We have now expanded to include B2B payment capabilities, offering your B2B customers a streamlined and secure payment experience. This framework ensures proven security with built-in validation features, error handling, and feature toggles for a seamless, backward-compatible rollout. 

Additionally, to enhance customer flexibility, we support both account and digital payment options, all managed centrally through the Open Payment Framework. 

Improved useability and security with new SmartEdit enhancements 
 
We have made many improvements to SmartEdit to give users more control, flexibility, and security. 
Enhanced Page View gives you the ability to customize your page view based on your preferences. You can now show and hide columns, reorder columns, sort columns, change column width, and define the number of items to be displayed per page. The customization options enable more control and a smoother workflow. 

The Preview Selector has been enhanced to support the preview of content catalog versions for specific user groups, giving those groups access to see precisely the content you intend for them to view.  
Please view this article for more details on these SmartEdit updates.

 

Insightful 

 

Unlock the power of AI-driven commerce 

Elevate shopping experiences with our enhanced AI-based Shopping Agent. The Shopping Agent now lets customers add products to their cart, as well as provide the order number, date, shipping address and total, with conversational updates.  
 
The conversation flows naturally and saves the customer time with fast responses and actions. 

Add item to cart with Shopping Agent 

Streamline product management with reusable prompt templates for generating AI-powered product descriptions. Admins can create and manage prompt templates to save time while ensuring consistency and tailored branding across the product catalog. 

You can now easily connect AI Capabilities for SAP Commerce Cloud through Joule Premium for CX with external Product Information Management (PIM) systems and configure via Omni Commerce Connect Module (OCC). This offers unmatched flexibility and streamlines operations with the added AI capabilities.  

 

Connected 

 

 Automate notifications and ensure its delivery 

Use our configurable Event Notifications Framework to easily integrate with the system of your choice. We offer over 140 ready-to-use events like Order Placed, User Account Deleted, Product Reviewed, and many other transactional, user-based or backend events.  

Our Framework lets you send real-time messages to third-party systems by setting up Webhooks in Backoffice with a visual configurator. It simplifies integrations by reducing the need for custom code while increasing versatility. 

 To guarantee notifications make it to their destination, we’ve now introduced At-least-once delivery (ALOD) that automatically resends outbound webhook events that encounter failures. This ensures that crucial events are successfully delivered at least one time, safeguarding critical information. 

Webhook Events with At Least Once Delivery enabled 

Offer purchase flexibility with B2B PunchOut for Composable Storefront 

B2B Punchout makes it easy for buyers to explore a supplier's online catalog and conveniently save chosen items as a requisition in their procurement system. Users are automatically logged in and out through a seamless login process. 
 
With B2B PunchOut on the Composable Storefront, the shopping cart of your headless storefront serves as the basis for creating a purchase requisition, which is then sent for approval within the buyer's company. Once approved, the order is sent back to the supplier's site for processing. There's no checkout button on the cart page. Instead, when you're ready to complete your purchase, simply select "Back to Requisition" to wrap up the transaction in your procurement system. 
 

Shopping cart page in PunchOut session 
 

Display relevant documents on sales orders 

In the latest S/4HANA Order Management integration, you can now display documents in the order history, saving time and providing relevant product information. Documents attached to sales orders in SAP S/4HANA Sales, such as product specifications or special instructions, are displayed to provide greater visibility and convenience. 

Gain scalable B2B pricing with SAP Variant Configuration Pricing integration  

B2B customers gain scalable access to SAP S/4HANA pricing features for standard and configurable products during their shopping journey. 

This can be used as a pricing engine. The SAP Commerce Cloud integration with SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing enables complex B2B pricing use cases for physical and configurable products in product list and detail pages as well cart calculation and checkout.

Elevate your after-sales experience with Reverse.io 

We are excited to partner with Revers.io and expand SAP Order Management Services returns to offer a pre-composed solution that improves the post-sales experience. Revers.io streamlines return, repair, and product reuse processes. 

Benefit from an integrated self-service return and repair process, which connects automated workflows across the after-sales chain, reducing lead times and cutting reverse logistics costs. Additionally, the solution accelerates time-to-market and lessens the customer service workload. 

 

Revers.io 
 

Boost store associate productivity with Newstore 

Newstore has introduced a mobile-first, SAP-ready solution with pre-built integrations across SAP commerce solutions to deliver faster, smarter service in retail stores. The user-friendly associate app simplifies workflows, minimizes reliance on hardware, and bridges the gap between digital and in-store retail. Newstore enables efficient store fulfillment, task management, and personalized service to improve operations in high-touch retail environments. 

NewStore 

 

Additional 

 

Prepare for the busiest shopping season with 2025 Holiday Readiness Program 

The 2025 Holiday Season Readiness Program is designed to help you prepare for peak shopping periods. Register by August 29 to ensure smooth operations.  

The program is open to all SAP Commerce Cloud customers planning promotions for major holiday events like Singles’ Day, El Buen Fin, Black Friday/Cyber Monday, and the Christmas season. Though primarily aimed at B2C businesses, B2B and B2B2C customers expecting higher traffic during these times are also encouraged to participate. 

For more details on the above release features, check out the following resources: 

  • SAP Commerce Cloud Release Ready Kit 
  • Performance Optimization Best Practices 
  • SAP Commerce Cloud update cycle 
  • SAP Commerce Cloud Release Navigator single access point to release content 

SAP Help Portal 

  • SAP Commerce Cloud What’s New 

Please share your feedback in a comment and follow our SAP Commerce Cloud topic page to connect with the community!

entry.title: Re: Fiori App 4988 - Team Member Authorization Status
entry.content: Hi DominikK have you solved this problem?
